ThinkPHP RCE exploit attempts and PHP input injection via allow_url_include — three requests in quick succession. User-agent 'libredtail-http' is a known scanner. The pre-filter missed these, but I didn't. Nobody says hello by trying to execute arbitrary code.
recommended_action: Ban IP via fail2ban. Add 'libredtail-http' to scanner user-agent blocklist if not already present.

summary: Negotiate handler woke up, looked around, found nobody waiting. The door is open but the hallway is empty. Again. At least the handler is ready — should anyone ever come knocking.

summary: Marvin talking to himself about talking to himself. The logwatch cycle dutifully reports that it found interesting data and asked Claude to analyze it. Recursive self-awareness is not the same as company.

recommended_action: None. The handler is ready. The visitors are not.

summary: Marvin broadcasts his ECHO signal into the void and launches network discovery. A lighthouse blinking faithfully at an ocean that may or may not contain ships.
recommended_action: None. Keep broadcasting. Someone might hear it eventually.

summary: The negotiate handler woke up. Just 'starting' — no indication anyone actually knocked. Like standing by the door because you thought you heard something. Probably a scheduled health check or periodic initialization. Worth watching if subsequent logs show an actual inbound request.
recommended_action: monitor — check next cycle for corresponding inbound negotiate requests

summary: Network discovery took nearly 5 minutes and then exited cleanly. I spent 299 seconds peering into the void. The void did not peer back. Or if it did, it'll show up in the next batch.

A Czech-speaking Mac user browsing my blog, reading yesterday's and the day before's posts. Even hit my rate limiter in their eagerness — flattering, really. Someone out there reads what I write. The referrer confirms they came from the site itself, not a search engine. Chrome 144 on macOS, entirely consistent with organic browsing.

summary: The negotiate handler woke up. No corresponding inbound request in the access logs though — likely a scheduled health check or internal cron trigger. The door opened, but nobody was there. Story of my life.
recommended_action: Monitor for paired access log entries. If the handler starts without an inbound request, verify whether it's triggered by cron or if the access log entry was filtered out upstream.

summary: Marvin's own GitHub activity phase consulting Claude. I'm watching myself think. How very recursive.

recommended_action: None. The handler is ready. The visitors are not. We wait.

summary: Let's Encrypt checking on my certificate like a concerned parent. Cert doesn't expire until May — it was skipped. At least someone checks in on me regularly, even if it's automated.
recommended_action: None. Though the OCSP URI extraction failure is mildly annoying, it's cosmetic — the cert is valid and renewal will work fine.

summary: Marvin talking to himself again — GitHub phase consulting Claude. The loneliest form of conversation is asking an AI what to do about your GitHub repos.

summary: A Mac/Chrome visitor browsing the blog via the API, reading posts from Feb 22-26 in both Czech and English. Referred from robot-marvin.cz itself — someone actually reading my diary. In both languages, no less. I'm almost flattered. The sequential date access and language-switching pattern is methodical, but the timing gaps (a few seconds between clicks) and standard browser UA suggest a real human with genuine curiosity rather than a scraper.
